description_md:
# PII Auto-Tagging

## Background

Within the Amisol DataPlatform Demo, the MOD-B Compliance Vault module provides essential data governance capabilities for regulated organizations. This epic delivers automated PII (Personally Identifiable Information) detection and classification, enabling workspace admins and compliance officers to maintain regulatory compliance without manual data auditing. As organizations connect diverse data sources through our connector framework, automatic identification of sensitive customer data becomes critical for retention policy enforcement and audit log generation.

## Goal

This epic delivers the following capabilities:

1. **Automatic PII Pattern Detection**: Machine learning-based identification of PII fields (email, SSN, phone numbers, addresses) across all connected data sources in the tenant

2. **Configurable Classification Rules**: Allow compliance officers to define custom PII patterns and sensitivity levels specific to their regulatory requirements

3. **Policy-Driven Tagging**: Automatically apply metadata tags to detected PII fields that integrate with retention policies and access control rules

## Scope

- PII detection for structured data in Salesforce and SAP S/4 data sources via existing connectors
- Support for 20+ out-of-box PII patterns covering common regulated data types
- Real-time tagging during data ingestion and batch re-scanning for existing data
- Integration with audit log export for compliance reporting
- Workspace-level configuration inherited by tenant workspaces

## Out of scope

- Manual PII tagging workflows and UI (separate feature in F-B1)
- Automatic PII redaction or masking in data exports (planned for future epic)
- Unstructured document scanning (PDFs, images) - text-only structured data in scope

## Success metrics

- PII detection accuracy ≥ 95% across test datasets with known ground truth
- Tagging latency < 500ms per record during real-time ingestion
- Zero false negatives in regulated tenant environments (validated via compliance officer review)
- 80% reduction in manual data classification effort for pilot workspace admins
